name: Tofu
description: Agentic demand-generation platform that generates and launches personalized B2B marketing campaigns across email, landing pages, and ads, consolidating point martech tools into one AI-driven workflow.

attributes.problemStatement: B2B marketing teams rely on a sprawl of disconnected point tools for personalization, landing pages, ad copy, and localization, slowing campaign velocity and fragmenting brand consistency.
attributes.productDescription: Tofu is an agentic demand-generation platform for B2B marketing teams that generates and launches hyper-personalized campaigns across email, landing pages, and paid ads from a shared brand and product knowledge base, positioned to consolidate martech point tools into one orchestration workflow.

founders.name: EJ Cho
founders.background: Named CEO in the company's February 2025 Series A financing announcement; leads GTM and product strategy.

founders.name: Elaine
founders.background: Named as a co-founder in the Series A financing announcement; full surname and background undisclosed in public sources.

founders.name: Honglei
founders.background: Named as a co-founder in the Series A financing announcement; full surname and background undisclosed in public sources.

name: Default
description: AI infrastructure platform for revenue teams that unifies the inbound GTM stack (data layer, scheduling, routing, enrichment, and an AI revenue agent called Dot) into one orchestration platform.

attributes.problemStatement: Revenue teams stitch together separate forms, scheduling, lead-routing, and enrichment tools, creating data fragmentation that slows inbound lead response and blocks agentic automation.
attributes.productDescription: Default provides a real-time GTM data layer connecting CRM and marketing-automation systems, an AI revenue agent (Dot) that plans and executes multi-step GTM work, and orchestration tools covering forms, scheduling, routing, and waterfall enrichment.

founders.name: Nico Ferreyra
founders.background: Named CEO in the company's Series A / agent-launch announcement; no further public operating background disclosed.

name: Thena
description: Customer support platform purpose-built for B2B teams that live in Slack, Microsoft Teams, chat, and email, bringing SLA policies, escalations, ownership rules, and AI routing directly into those channels.

disruptionMechanisms: Workflow-native delivery inside existing collaboration tools
attributes.problemStatement: B2B customers and support teams increasingly communicate through Slack and other collaboration tools, but standalone helpdesk software forces support workflows outside those channels.
attributes.productDescription: Thena brings enterprise-grade support workflows -- SLA policies, escalations, ownership rules, AI-based routing, and an AI web-chat agent -- directly into Slack, Microsoft Teams, in-app chat, and email rather than a standalone ticketing tool.

founders.name: Mike Molinet
founders.background: Founding team member per company-funding coverage dating to Thena's 2022 founding.

founders.name: Govind Kavaturi
founders.background: Founding team member per the same sourcing; role details not further broken out publicly.

founders.name: Unmukt Raizada
founders.background: Founding team member per the same sourcing; role details not further broken out publicly.

founders.name: Satyavrat Bondre
founders.background: Founding team member per the same sourcing; role details not further broken out publicly.

name: Dock
description: AI revenue-enablement platform built around client-facing digital workspaces -- digital sales rooms, onboarding plans, and content libraries -- connected to Dock AI, which generates real-time guidance from CRM and call-transcript data.

productCategoryPath: Digital sales rooms and buyer workspaces
attributes.problemStatement: Sales, customer success, and enablement teams manage buyer-facing content through disconnected decks, emails, and file shares, giving buyers a fragmented and hard-to-navigate experience.
attributes.productDescription: Dock combines digital sales rooms, customer onboarding plans, client portals, and a content library with Dock AI, an engine that connects CRM data, call transcripts, the content library, and buyer engagement data to generate real-time guidance.

founders.name: Alex Kracov
founders.background: Former VP of Marketing and third employee at Lattice; founded Dock in 2021 after building an internal customer-facing portal at Lattice.

founders.name: Luc
founders.background: Former Lattice employee who worked with Kracov; full surname undisclosed in public sources.

founders.name: Victor
founders.background: Early Lattice engineer; full surname undisclosed in public sources.

name: Kula
description: AI-native applicant tracking system that unifies sourcing, recruiting CRM, screening, interview scheduling, interview intelligence, and analytics into one workflow.

attributes.problemStatement: Recruiting teams stitch together a legacy ATS with separate sourcing, CRM, scheduling, and interview-note tools, creating data fragmentation and manual overhead across the hiring workflow.
attributes.productDescription: Kula is an AI-native ATS unifying sourcing, a recruiting CRM, screening, interview scheduling, interview intelligence, and analytics, integrating with 100+ third-party systems, with AI-powered resume screening, an AI interview notetaker, and a conversational analytics dashboard.

founders.name: Achuthanand Tanjore Ravi
founders.background: Previously held recruiting operating roles at Stripe, Uber, and Freshworks per the company's seed-funding announcement.

name: Metaview
description: AI interview-intelligence platform: an AI notetaker captures interviews, generates structured reports, and feeds a broader hiring workflow that extends to job-post generation and candidate search.

attributes.problemStatement: Interview outcomes historically live as unstructured notes and inconsistent scoring, making hiring decisions hard to audit, compare, or improve over time.
attributes.productDescription: Metaview's AI notetaker captures interviews, generates structured reports, and feeds a hiring workflow that has expanded into job-post generation and candidate search, having captured and analyzed more than 3 million interviews across its customer base.

founders.name: Siadhal Magos
founders.background: Previously at Uber and Palantir; co-founded Metaview in 2018.

founders.name: Shahriar Tajbakhsh
founders.background: Previously at Uber and Palantir; leads technical development of Metaview's interview-intelligence platform.

name: Campfire
description: AI-native enterprise resource planning platform automating core accounting workflows -- bank reconciliation, invoicing, and revenue accounting -- for mid-market and enterprise organizations, positioned as a modern replacement for NetSuite.

attributes.problemStatement: Legacy ERP systems built in the 1990s require lengthy implementations and slow, manual month-end close processes that fast-growing companies find hard to tolerate.
attributes.productDescription: Campfire automates core accounting workflows -- bank reconciliation, invoicing, and revenue accounting -- for mid-market and enterprise finance teams, with a disclosed customer example cutting month-end close from 15 days to 3 days after migrating from NetSuite.

founders.name: John Glasgow
founders.background: Launched Campfire in 2023 to replace legacy 1990s-era ERP accounting software with an LLM-powered alternative, per TechCrunch's reporting. Additional co-founders, if any, are not identified in public sources.

name: Doss
description: AI-native ERP and operations cloud (DossARP, the Adaptive Resource Platform) unifying inventory management, order processing, accounting, and production for mid-market physical-goods companies.

attributes.problemStatement: Mid-market physical-goods companies outgrow spreadsheets and basic accounting tools but face multi-year implementations and high failure rates when adopting traditional ERP systems like NetSuite.
attributes.productDescription: Doss unifies inventory management, order processing, accounting, and production into a single, composable AI-native ERP system designed for both humans and AI agents to reconfigure, targeting mid-market physical-goods companies with implementations as fast as two days.

founders.name: Wiley Jones
founders.background: Co-authored the company's Series A announcement; described prior ERP-systems experience before founding Doss.

founders.name: Arnav
founders.background: Co-founder per the Series A announcement; full surname and background undisclosed in public sources.

name: Sequence
description: AI-native revenue-automation platform for B2B SaaS and fintech companies covering CPQ, billing, and revenue recognition in one connected quote-to-cash workflow.

targetCustomers: B2B SaaS finance and RevOps teams
attributes.problemStatement: RevOps and finance teams manage custom deals and modern usage-based or hybrid pricing models through spreadsheets and fragmented billing tools that cannot keep pace with pricing complexity.
attributes.productDescription: Sequence covers CPQ (quoting), billing, and revenue recognition in one connected quote-to-cash workflow, built to natively support usage-based, hybrid, and seat-based pricing models used by fast-growing B2B SaaS and fintech companies.

founders.name: Riya Grover
founders.background: Named CEO in the Series A financing announcement; leads product and company strategy.

founders.name: Eamon
founders.background: Named as a co-founder in the Series A financing announcement; full surname and background undisclosed in public sources.

founders.name: Enda
founders.background: Named as a co-founder in the Series A financing announcement; full surname and background undisclosed in public sources.

name: Gradient Labs
description: AI agents for customer operations at regulated financial-services companies -- dispute resolution, KYC/KYB, lending, collections, onboarding, and insurance claims -- built with financial-services-specific regulatory guardrails.

disruptionMechanisms: Regulatory-native design versus horizontal AI support
attributes.problemStatement: Regulated financial-services companies cannot safely deploy horizontal AI customer-support tools because compliance, audit-trail, and guardrail requirements (FCA Consumer Duty, PSD2, EU AI Act) were not built into general-purpose products.
attributes.productDescription: Gradient Labs' AI agents handle dispute resolution, KYC/KYB, lending, collections, onboarding, insurance claims, and general customer service across email, text, and voice, carrying financial-services-specific regulatory guardrails and SOC 2 Type II / GDPR compliance.

founders.name: Dimitri Masin
founders.background: Founded Gradient Labs in 2023 after working at Monzo as an early employee, where he met his co-founders while helping build the bank at scale.

founders.name: Neal Lathia
founders.background: Former Monzo colleague of Masin; co-founded Gradient Labs in 2023.

founders.name: Danai Antoniou
founders.background: Former Monzo colleague of Masin; co-founded Gradient Labs in 2023.
